# @overflow-aware-list/core

> Framework-agnostic core logic for measuring container width/height and computing which items in a list fit within a fixed boundary. Zero dependencies. Used internally by `@overflow-aware-list/react` and `@overflow-aware-list/svelte`; also consumable directly for custom framework integrations.

- GitHub: https://github.com/rai-aashish/overflow-aware-list/tree/main/packages/core
- React demo: https://rai-aashish.github.io/overflow-aware-list/react/
- Svelte demo: https://rai-aashish.github.io/overflow-aware-list/svelte

## Overview

The core package exposes two things:

1. **Pure computation functions** — given pixel sizes, return how many items fit and which slice to show.
2. **`OverflowListController`** — a class that wires `ResizeObserver` instances to a container element, off-screen item elements, and a "more" indicator element, then calls a callback whenever the visible/hidden split changes.

The controller never touches the DOM beyond reading sizes — all rendering is left to the framework wrapper.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @overflow-aware-list/core
```

## Key concepts

### Two-render-pass pattern

Framework wrappers render items twice:

- **Visible container** — clipped with `overflow: hidden`, shows only `visibleItems`.
- **Off-screen measurement layer** — `position: absolute; visibility: hidden; left: -9999px` — renders *all* items at natural size so the controller can measure them.

The controller reads sizes from the off-screen layer and tells the framework how many items fit.

### sliceFrom

- `"start"` (default) — show first N items, hide the rest, put the "more" indicator at the **end**.
- `"end"` — show last N items (ideal for breadcrumbs/paths), hide earlier items, put the "more" indicator at the **start**.

### measured flag

`OverflowListState.measured` is `false` until the first recompute completes. Use it to avoid a flash of incorrectly-sized content on first render.

## Types

```ts
type Direction = "horizontal" | "vertical";
type SliceFrom = "start" | "end";

interface OverflowListState<T> {
  visibleItems: T[];
  hiddenItems: T[];
  isOverflowing: boolean;
  /** Index into `items` where the visible slice begins. Always 0 when sliceFrom="start". */
  startIndex: number;
  /** False until the first measurement completes. */
  measured: boolean;
}

interface OverflowListOptions<T> {
  items: T[];
  direction?: Direction;       // default: "horizontal"
  sliceFrom?: SliceFrom;       // default: "start"
  /** Called synchronously whenever visible/hidden state changes. */
  onStateChange: (state: OverflowListState<T>) => void;
}
```

## OverflowListController

### Constructor

```ts
const controller = new OverflowListController<T>(options: OverflowListOptions<T>)
```

### Element registration

```ts
// Register the visible/clip container. Observes it for size changes.
controller.mountContainer(el: HTMLElement | null): void

// Register the off-screen measurement element for item at `index`.
controller.registerItemElement(index: number, el: HTMLElement | null): void

// Register the off-screen measurement element for the "more" indicator.
controller.registerMoreElement(el: HTMLElement | null): void
```

Pass `null` to any of these to unregister and disconnect the observer.

### Updating options

```ts
// Call when items, direction, or sliceFrom props change.
controller.updateOptions(partial: {
  items?: T[];
  direction?: Direction;
  sliceFrom?: SliceFrom;
}): void
```

Only triggers a recompute if a value actually changed.

### Recompute

```ts
// Schedules a recompute on the next animation frame (batches rapid calls).
// Called automatically by ResizeObserver callbacks.
controller.recompute(): void  // synchronous, immediate
```

### Cleanup

```ts
// Disconnects all ResizeObservers and cancels any pending RAF. Safe to call multiple times.
controller.destroy(): void
```

## Pure computation functions

These are exported for testing or custom integrations.

```ts
// Returns the inner content size of `el` along `direction` (excludes padding + border).
getAvailableSize(el: HTMLElement, direction: Direction): number

// Returns the flex/grid gap between children along `direction`. Returns 0 for "normal".
getGap(el: HTMLElement, direction: Direction): number

// Given item pixel sizes, returns how many fit in `availableSize`.
// When items overflow, `moreSize` pixels are reserved for the "more" indicator.
computeVisibleCount(
  availableSize: number,
  sizes: number[],
  moreSize: number,
  gap: number
): number

// Splits `items` into visible/hidden slices given `visibleCount` and `sliceFrom`.
computeSlice<T>(
  items: T[],
  visibleCount: number,
  sliceFrom: SliceFrom
): { visibleItems: T[]; hiddenItems: T[]; startIndex: number; isOverflowing: boolean }
```

## Custom framework integration example

```ts
import { OverflowListController } from "@overflow-aware-list/core";

// 1. Create controller
const controller = new OverflowListController({
  items: myItems,
  direction: "horizontal",
  sliceFrom: "start",
  onStateChange: (state) => {
    // re-render with state.visibleItems / state.hiddenItems
  },
});

// 2. After mounting DOM elements:
controller.mountContainer(containerEl);
items.forEach((_, i) => controller.registerItemElement(i, offScreenItemEls[i]));
controller.registerMoreElement(offScreenMoreEl);

// 3. On prop changes:
controller.updateOptions({ items: newItems });

// 4. On unmount:
controller.destroy();
```

## SSR behaviour

When `ResizeObserver` and `requestAnimationFrame` are unavailable (server-side), the controller skips observation and falls back to a synchronous no-op. `measured` remains `false`. Framework wrappers should render the full item list on the server so the page is usable before JS hydrates.
